title: Ultimate GBP Optimization Guide
content:
Created By: Local SEO Club (If you enjoy this free resource, get more free SEO related
gifts to your inbox each month at: https://local-seo-club.com/free-gifts/)
The Ultimate Google Business Profile
Optimization Guide
Introduction
Google Business Profile (GBP) is a vital tool for local businesses looking to enhance
their visibility on Google Search and Maps. By thoroughly optimizing your GBP, you can
significantly improve your local SEO, attract more customers, and outperform your
competitors. This guide will walk you through every necessary step, from basic setup to
advanced strategies, providing you with a comprehensive approach to mastering your
GBP.
1. Google My Business (GMB) Setup and Optimization
1.1 Categories
●
Primary Category: Select a primary category that best describes your business.
This category will heavily influence which search results your business appears
in, so choose wisely based on your core services or products. Learn more about
selecting the right category.
●
Secondary Categories: You can add additional categories that cover other aspects
of your business. For example, if you own a bakery that also offers catering
services, you might choose "Carpet Cleaning Service" as your primary category
and "Upholstery Cleaning" as a secondary one.
1.2 Service Areas (For Service Area Businesses - SABs)


●
Defining Service Areas: If your business operates in multiple locations or offers
services at the customer’s location, specify these areas within your GBP. This
helps Google understand where your business is relevant, increasing your
chances of appearing in searches within those regions.
●
City-Specific Listings: Create city-specific pages on your website and link them to
your GBP to enhance your visibility in local searches. For example, if you serve
both New York City and Brooklyn, create dedicated service pages for each area.
1.3 Reviews Management
●
Collecting Reviews: Make it easy for satisfied customers to leave reviews by
providing them with direct links. Consistently gathering reviews will improve your
online reputation and boost your ranking in local searches. Use tools like
Whitespark to help with this process.
●
Responding to Reviews: Engage with all reviews, whether positive or negative.
Responding to reviews shows that you value customer feedback and care about
customer satisfaction. This engagement can also positively influence potential
customers.
●
Leveraging Reviews in Google Posts: Feature positive reviews in your Google
Posts by sharing a link to the review and adding relevant content. This can be
enhanced by including a geo-tagged image, although it’s optional and can depend
on your strategy. Shortened links using tools like Bitly can be used to share
reviews.
1.4 Google Posts
●
Regular Posting Strategy: Keep your GBP fresh and engaging by posting updates,
promotions, or events regularly. Ensure each post contains relevant keywords to
improve its SEO potential. Google My Business Post Guidelines offer insights into
what works best.
●
Optional Geo-Tagged Images: Including geo-tagged images can enhance the local
relevance of your posts, though it's optional. These images help tie your content
to specific locations, potentially improving local search visibility. How to geo-tag
your images.
●
Keyword Variation: Diversify the keywords used in each post to cover a broader
range of search queries, improving your visibility across different search terms.
2. Google Properties Integration
Google’s ecosystem of tools can be leveraged to boost your GBP and overall SEO
strategy. Here’s how you can use them effectively.
2.1 Google Drive Stack


●
Google Docs, Sheets, and Slides: Create a stack of documents that are interlinked
and related to your business. For instance, a Google Doc outlining your services
can link to a Google Sheet tracking customer testimonials, which in turn can link
to a Google Slide deck showcasing a case study. This internal linking within
Google’s ecosystem can improve your online authority.
●
Google MyMaps: Use MyMaps to create custom maps of your service areas or
customer locations, and embed these on your website. This can improve your
relevance in local searches. Learn to use Google MyMaps.
●
Google Sites: If applicable, create a Google Site as an additional web property.
Link it back to your main website and GBP to create a network of interlinked
content that Google recognizes as part of your digital footprint.
2.2 Google Photos
●
Optional Geo-Tagged Images: Regularly upload high-quality photos of your
business, products, and services to Google Photos and your GBP. While
geo-tagging these images can enhance your local SEO, it is optional. Ensure your
photos are high quality and represent your business well. Best practices for
business photos.
2.3 YouTube and Videos
●
Creating Video Content: Produce videos that showcase your business, such as
customer testimonials, product demos, or behind-the-scenes footage. Upload
these to YouTube and link them to your GBP and website. Geo-tagging videos is
also an option that can help localize your content.
●
YouTube Optimization: Optimize your YouTube videos with proper titles,
descriptions, and tags to increase visibility in both Google and YouTube search
results. Learn more about YouTube SEO.
3. Website Integration with Google Business Profile
Your website is the cornerstone of your online presence and should work harmoniously
with your GBP to maximize your SEO potential.
3.1 Embedding Google Maps
●
Map Placement: Embed a Google Map of your business location on your website.
The footer is a common place for this, but it can also be placed on your contact or
about page. This not only helps users find you but also signals to Google that
your website and GBP are related. How to embed Google Maps.
●
Schema Markup: Implement JSON-LD schema markup on your site to help search
engines better understand your content. This can include business information,


products, and services, which can then appear in rich snippets on search results.
Use Google’s Structured Data Markup Helper.
3.2 Site Structure
●
Service Pages: Create individual pages on your website for each service you offer.
These pages should be optimized for relevant keywords and linked to
corresponding service categories in your GBP. This alignment strengthens your
SEO.
●
City-Specific Pages: If your business serves multiple cities, create dedicated
pages for each location. These should be optimized with local keywords and
linked to your GBP. This helps Google understand the geographic areas you
cover. Tips for local SEO.
3.3 Image Optimization
●
Optional Geo-Tagging and EXIF Data: While optional, geo-tagging images and
including relevant EXIF data (like location and date) can provide Google with
additional context, potentially improving local SEO. Ensure images are also
optimized with alt text and are compressed for faster loading times. How to
optimize images for SEO.
3.4 Rich Website Content
●
Comprehensive Content Strategy: Your website should offer rich, informative
content that answers potential customer questions and showcases your expertise.
This can include blog posts, FAQs, service descriptions, and customer
testimonials.
●
Embedding Google Maps in Content: Consider embedding Google Maps in blog
posts or service pages that discuss locations or events. This not only enhances
user experience but also reinforces your business's connection to these areas.
4. Leveraging Rich Media and Citations
Utilize rich media and citations to build your business’s online authority and credibility,
improving your local SEO in the process.
4.1 Google Photos
●
Optional Geo-Tagged Images: Upload images to your GBP regularly. While
geo-tagging is optional, doing so can enhance your local relevance. Make sure the
images are clear, well-composed, and relevant to your business. Google’s
guidelines on business photos.


4.2 Citations
●
NAP Consistency: Ensure that your business’s Name, Address, and Phone
number (NAP) are consistent across all online directories. Inconsistent
information can lead to confusion for both customers and search engines,
negatively impacting your local SEO.
●
Local Directory Listings: Aim to list your business on a wide range of local
directories—at least matching the main competitors if possible. This helps solidify
your online presence and can improve your local rankings. Tools like BrightLocal
can help manage your citations efficiently.
4.3 Rich Media Citations
●
Videos and YouTube: Upload videos related to your business on YouTube,
ensuring they are optimized for SEO with appropriate titles, descriptions, and
tags. Videos can be a powerful tool for improving your search visibility, both on
Google and YouTube.
●
Slide Decks and Presentations: Use platforms like Google Slides or SlideShare to
share presentations related to your business. These can include service
explanations, case studies, or industry insights. Embed these on your website and
link them to your GBP to create a network of content that enhances your online
authority.
5. Advanced Strategies
To stand out in local search results, consider implementing advanced strategies that go
beyond the basics.
5.1 Press Releases
●
Effective Press Releases: Craft and distribute press releases for significant
business updates, such as launching new services, winning awards, or opening
new locations. Ensure these releases are SEO-optimized and include links to your
website and GBP. How to write an SEO-friendly press release.
●
Local Media Outreach: Reach out to local news outlets and blogs to cover your
press releases. A mention in a reputable local publication can significantly boost
your credibility and visibility.
5.2 Google About Me Page
●
Crafting Your Business Story: Use your Google About Me page to tell your
business’s story. Highlight your mission, values, and what makes your business
unique. Include links to your GBP and website to further strengthen your online
presence. Edit your Google About Me page.


5.3 Utilizing Web 2.0 Properties
●
Building Web 2.0 Links: Create profiles and blogs on platforms like WordPress,
Tumblr, and Medium that link back to your GBP and main website. These links can
improve your SEO by creating a broader online footprint.
●
Content Syndication: Share content across various Web 2.0 properties to reach a
wider audience and drive traffic back to your primary web assets.
6. Continuous Monitoring and Improvement
Optimizing your GBP and overall online presence is an ongoing process. Regularly
review your strategies and adapt to changes in Google's algorithms and local SEO
trends.
●
Regular GBP Audits: Periodically audit your GBP to ensure all information is
accurate and up to date. This includes checking business hours, contact details,
and service offerings.
●
Staying Updated on Local SEO Trends: Keep abreast of the latest local SEO trends
and best practices by following industry blogs and forums, such as Moz’s Local
SEO blog.
●
Tracking Performance: Use tools like Google Analytics and Google Search
Console to monitor the performance of your GBP and website, and make
data-driven decisions to improve your strategy.
Created By: Local SEO Club (If you enjoyed this free resource, get more free SEO related
gifts sent to your inbox each month at: https://local-seo-club.com/free-gifts/)

---

learn how to boost your click-through rate through CTR
manipulation. 
If that sounds like you, then you’ve come to the right place. In this
guide, you’ll learn everything you need to know about CTR
manipulation, how it works, and how you can get started today, in a
simple, step-by-step process.
You’ll learn exactly the right way to increase your website click-
through rate without hurting your website’s SEO.

he ultimate goal of many

engagement. The more traffic to your website, the higher your
chance of converting mere web visitors to leads and to sales.
In general terms, click-through rate or CTR is the percentage of
people who click on your web pages or links in comparison to those
who don’t.
CTR is what all website owners and digital marketers want to
happen to any of their web pages. You want your website to gain
more visibility on search engines like Google or Bing so you can have
more people clicking on your web pages with a view of engaging
them with your content and possibly converting them to your
customers.
Your digital marketing success (whether you’re using paid search
method or SEO) is determined by how many people are visiting your
website or social media pages, and whether or not they’re taking the
intended action after engaging with your page or content.


02
Besides, CTR is an important factor in page rankings on Google. Having a lot
of people clicking and engaging with your web pages is a positive sign that
tells Google that your page is offering value.
Because CTR seems important in SEO, it shouldn’t come as a surprise to you
that people have started devising tricks to manipulate the search engine in
order to get better rankings.
Although CTR manipulation raises some ethical questions among SEOs, it
can be a legit strategy that you can use without being penalized if you’re
smart about it.
www.rankfortress.com


03
C
clicks that your web pages or site receives divided by the number
of times it appears in the search engine for your particular
keyword. For example, if you had 10 clicks and 100 impressions
(the number of times your web pages come up for specific search
queries), then your CTR would be 10%.
CTR can be used to measure how well your keywords are
performing in the search engine. A high CTR is a positive
indication that users find your page helpful and relevant to their
search queries. In paid search or pay per click, CTR contributes
significantly to your keyword’s expected click-through rate, which
is a key component of Ad Rank on Google.
Many studies over the past years have sought to gauge CTR
based on search engine rankings, and have ultimately found that
higher placement or ranking in search engine result pages
(SERPs) are correct with higher click-through rates.
It should be noted that a good CTR is relative to what industry
you’re in and how competitive your keywords are.

This is how it works…
Google and other search engines utilize bots to crawl all the webpages
on the web, moving from one site to another and collecting information
about each individual page and adding them to a large library-like
index list. Every piece of information about the crawled pages is
analyzed, making it easy for the search engine to pull up information
users are looking for at a particular time.
When a user types in a keyword or uses voice command on Google, the
search engine will return a list of results that’s relevant to the user’s
search query. If your web page shows up, the user will determine
whether or not to click on it.
While Google tries to provide users with the most accurate and
relevant results based on their search terms, users will decide which
page to click and which one to ignore. In most cases, users are looking
for something specific and relevant to their questions. That said, they
tend to click on the first page or link that most closely matches their
search intent.
Oftentimes, a combination of catchy, relevant titles and attention-
grabbing descriptions will do the trick. But beyond writing good titles
and enticing descriptions, it’s important to provide the user with
valuable and high-quality content to keep them engaged and drive
them along down through the sales funnel.
www.rankfortress.com

05

MANIPULATION?

lick-through rate manipulation
lick-through rate manipulation
or organic CTR manipulation
or organic CTR manipulation
is an SEO tactic where website owners, or digital 
marketers generate fake clicks towards their websites to trick the
search engines, including Google into thinking that your site is more
popular than it actually is.
CTR is an important ranking factor on Google. When you have more
people clicking your pages or links in the search results, it sends a
signal to the search that your content is valuable and helpful to users.
You can use CTR manipulation to boost and increase your site’s
ranking through the search results, allowing you to leapfrog your
competitions in terms of high ranking position for your intended
keywords or search terms.
Probably because the concept is phrased, a lot of people see CTR
manipulation is a complex, daunting task, but it doesn’t have to be.
Many people even do almost everything single time they post a new
blog on their website without knowing it.
Each time you share a blog post to your social media platform or to an
email list and it starts receiving clicks, you’re practicing a form of CTR
manipulation. However, the most effective form of CTR manipulation
is one where there’s actually searches and clicking in the search result.


06
might have a negative impact on their website in the long run?
Yes, CTR manipulation works.
But as with anything digital marketing, the devil is in the details.
Let’s face it, search engine optimization is hard and it can be frustrating sometimes.
With the enormous scale of information present on the web, returning results that
are relevant to the user’s search queries would be almost impossible without having
a system that helps in sorting it.
Google uses over 200 ranking factors in deciding which web pages show up first for
a particular keyword or search term. Scarily, Google continues to update its
algorithm at unprecedented rates, making it difficult for website owners to keep up
with their ranking factors.
In the end, the ultimate goal of any SEO effort or campaign is to drive traffic,
increase CTR and boost conversion rate.
With CTR manipulation, you don’t need to worry over having to wait endlessly for
Google to find your web pages worthy before you can rank and push your website
to the top of search results.

hile many people don’t seem to question

The first ever publicized case study of click-through rate was in
2014 when Rand Fishkin, founder of Moz, conducted an
experiment using his Twitter audience to demonstrate the
effectiveness of the technique.
As at 6:03PM, his targeted site ranked 7th for the target search
term or keyword. He tweeted via his Twitter account, asking his
followers to search the specific search phrase and follow the link
to his website.
By 9:01PM (same day), his site’s ranking has jumped from 7th
position to the first position for the same search term. Similar
case studies have been carried out by other industry experts like
Chris Palmer. Websites like Searchseo.io have also published
numerous case studies proving the impact of CTR manipulation
on search engine optimization. 
Rand’s case study was done more roughly seven years ago, and
lots of improvements have been made to the search engine since
then. Today, Google takes days and sometimes weeks before
they allow changes to websites to reflect. But while the result
may not be instantaneous like it was in 2014, you can rest
assured that your efforts will yield good results if you do it
correctly.
Keep in mind that Google and other major search engines frown
upon CTR manipulation or any practice that attempts to
undermine their search algorithms. This means if you have to do
it, then you should be very smart with it, and most importantly
consider the organic method of doing it.

es, Google uses click-through rate as one of

the ranking signals.
The question regarding whether Google uses  click-through 
rate as one of its ranking factors was a debate that stretched for a long time
in the SEO industry.
This was complicated by the fact that Google and other prominent search
engines continue to keep their ranking algorithms as top secrets. SEO, on the
other hand, have always relied on research and insights from Google to
understand how search engines’ algorithms function to improve the ranking
and visibility of clients’ websites.
The Wall Street Journal via the U.S. Freedom of Information Act was able to
obtain an integral Google document that confirms that Google actually uses
CTR as a ranking factor.
A piece of excerpt from the document is quoted as saying:
“In addition, click data (the website links on which a user actually clicks) is
important for evaluating the quality of the search results page. As Google’s
former chief of search quality Udi Manber testified:
“The ranking itself is affected by the click data. If we discover that, for a
particular query, hypothetically, 80 percent of people click on Result No. 2
and 10 percent click on Result No. 1, after a while we figure out, well,
probably Result 2 is the one people want. So we’ll switch it.”
Likewise, testimony from Google's founders Sergey Brin and Eric Schmidt
confirms that apart from using click data to get feedback on whether
Google's search algorithms are providing its users high quality results, the
click data also serves other purposes, including ranking well on the SERPs.

Use Crowd Searches From Your Audience

One of the common issues with many websites is what SEOs call
keyword cannibalization. That’s a big word, right? So what does it
mean?
Keyword cannibalization occurs when you have a number of
identical keywords spread across multiple pages on your website.
As a result, search engines like Google become confused regarding
which page to rank higher for the specific keyword.
This means that sometimes Google will give higher ranking to the
web page that’s not adding any serious value to your business.
Another possibility is that it decrease the rank all the pages that
have the keywords.
The first thing you want to do is check if any of your keywords is
having that issue. For example, if your roofing repair business has
the website name, “roofrepairatlanta.com,” and you want to look
for keyword cannibalization for “emergency roof repair,” you would
search for oofrepairatlanta.com, emergency roof repair, and
similar variations.
2. Fix Any Keyword Cannibalization
Google will pull up a list of results, but are the pages you
want? If the emergency roof repair post you published
several months ago ranking higher than your most
recent and most important one?
Is Google ranking your blog posts higher than your core
service page? If so, this would be a good example of
keyword cannibalization. 
Fortunately, there are a few ways you can correct that
and start ranking for the important pages on your
website.

Merge Similar Content
Merge Similar Content

talking about similar topics, such as a web page talking about how to choose a local
roofing company for emergency roofing repair and another that lists telltale signs
that your roof needs repair, you may want to put all this content in on a single page.
If done correctly, merging content will help Google focus on only one page for a
target keyword and increase your website’s ranking and visibility on the search
engine.
Delete Irrelevant Content
Delete Irrelevant Content
This could be a hard decision to make, but deleting a web page may be the right
thing to do in certain situations. This is especially necessary if the web page is no
longer relevant to your website.
If your business has grown, and you still have an older, less professional blog post
using certain important keywords, and is ranking higher than your desired content,
then complete deletion should be considered. It would change anything if the
context no longer exists.

This is probably the hardest way of addressing keyword

Google considers when ranking web pages in search results, among
many others, is the quality and quantity of your backlinks. Backlinks
are those external links linking back to your content.
After scanning your website for keyword cannibalization, you may find
that one of the reasons your less important content is ranking higher
than your more relevant content is because other websites keep
linking back to it.
If they’re convinced, they’ll replace the old links you
don’t want with the new ones that you’re seeking to
rank higher for. This way, you’ll be able to strengthen
your SEO ranking in the search results.

Writing a custom, descriptive meta description for each of your web pages or blog
posts is one of the most effective ways to improve your organic CTR and drive
traffic to your website. Meta descriptions are short pieces of writing that appear
immediately under web page titles in the search results. It provides information
about what your page is about. It’s a great way to provide both Google and users
about the content of your content.
However, meta description is often one of the most overlooked components when
publishing new content on websites. If you don’t create a clear meta description,
Google will automatically take a portion of your content and use it for the
description. Often, these kinds of descriptions will not make much sense.

your web page but creatively include the keywords you’re hoping to rank for. A
compelling, user-focused meta description will have an immensely positive impact
on your CTRs.
When writing a meta description, put thought into why people are checking for your
keyword or key phrase in the first place, and make sure your description is relevant
to their search intent.

6. Structure your content for Featured
6. Structure your content for Featured
Snippets
Snippets
Featured snippets are those rich search results that appear as boxes
of content in the SERPs. They’re clusters of information that attempt
to provide answers related to the user’s query. The goal of featuring in
rich snippets is to take up as much space in the SERPs as possible. You
want to get the user’s eyes glued to your content as much as possible.
There’s no one clear way to be in featured snippets. However, expert
SEOs have pointed out that structuring your content in a particular
way can increase your chances of featuring in the visual search listing.
Some of the ways to structure your content for rich snippets include
adding tables, reviews and FAQs.
One of the best ways to secure a spot in Google rich results is through
structured data implementation. The popular search engines like
Google have developed a project called Schema.org where
webmasters can find all structured data markup supported by the
search engines.
Schema markup is the language of search engines, using a
unique semantic vocabulary to clearly provide information to
search engines to understand your content. You can use
Schema.org to find the markup you need for a particular
page. For example, if you run a local roofing repair company,
you could show the image of the projects you’ve completed in
the past, customer reviews, and the core benefits you offer in
your snippet.


36
www.rankfortress.com
Webmasters use aggregate ratings or reviews markup to get star rating features
next to their URLs in Google organic search. Note that Google will not show your star
ratings if the ratings are of your own company, instead they display reviews from
your GMB. Adding a review schema has a huge impact on CTR. They make search
results compelling and provide the user with a high level of trust to buy your
products or use your services.
Common types of schema include:
Another common structured data you notice in the search engine results pages is
frequently asked questions (FAQs). This schema markup produces rich results in the
SERPs much like a featured snippet. Providing answers to common questions that
users are asking often results in a higher organic CTR.
Review Schema
FAQ Schema
Article Schema
Using structured data for your news, blog and article page can enhance the
appearance of your content in Google search results. When combined with Google
Accelerated Mobile Pages (AMP), the Article structured data can help your content
to appear in many rich results, such as Carousel and Top Stories.
The required properties for article structured data are headlines, image, author,
date published and publisher. The headline shouldn’t exceed 11 characters due to
the limited space for the rich result. Multiple image formats and styles are
supported, including responsive images. The publisher’s logo can also be added.
Using a different variation of markups, where applicable, can help bring more
visibility to your listing in the SERPs. And the longer users get to see your results, the
more likely they tend to click on your links. When users see your results in the SERPs,
they see not only the FAQs and review scores, but also prices, image and product
features. This goes a long way in prompting them to visit your website.

WordPress editor, select a word or group of words you want to add a link to, and
then use the link too to paste the referring content URL. You can also search for
the content on your website.
SEO best practice for Internal linking
Use a descriptive anchor text. Use anchor text that correctly describes the
content of the page you’re taking the reader to.
Use keyword-rich anchor text: Using the right keyword in your anchor text
helps the Google bot to understand the connection between the pages, and
possibly improve your ranking for that keyword.
Avoid adding internal links to your first paragraph or when you need the
reader to keep reading, buy a product, or take an action. It will distract them
from taking the intended actions
Make sure your link is related to the anchor text
Use a reasonable number of links. Although there’s no specific rule on how
many internal links to add to a page, SEOs believe adding too many on-page
links (above 100) can hinder rather than help your website. 


45
www.rankfortress.com
Using the right call to actions (CTAs) where necessary is one of
the great ways to provide users with quality experience. You
want to prompt them to take action and guide them every step
of the way throughout their visit to your website. CTAs enable
the users to navigate the website seamlessly.
It’s like when you visit a museum for the first time. You tend to
enjoy it much better if there’s a museum tour guide to show you
different sections within the site. If there’s none, or a brochure
with a map, or direction signs on the walls, there are chances
you might get lost or miss a lot of things you would have
otherwise experienced.
5. Guide Visitors Through Your Websites With
5. Guide Visitors Through Your Websites With
CTAs
CTAs
CTAs are your best bet to help visitors maximize their time
on your website.
Make sure your CTA clearly conveys the actual action
you want the visitor to take. Do you want them to
subscribe to your newsletter or download your free e-
book? The visitors will not bother about any of this
unless you ask them to.


46
www.rankfortress.com
Some of the common CTAs include:
👉Order now”
👉“Read more”
👉“Get started”
👉“Subscribe to our newsletter”
👉“Plan your trip”
👉“Start your website today”
👉“Talk to our expert today”
👉“Download the guide”
👉“Show me how to get started”
👉“Start learning today”
Make sure you clearly guide the visitor on the specific action you want them
to take.


47
www.rankfortress.com
Do you have old but relevant posts related to your new post and may
provide additional help to visitors? If yes, then don’t hesitate to share it
with your audience.
Related posts is one of the most effective strategies to make readers
continue to browse your content while providing premium experience to
the readers.
For example, if you already have an article about the “how to choose the
right roof repair company” and your new article is on “common signs of
roof damage”, you can add the existing blog to the related post section.
The two posts are related because someone looking for the common signs
of roof damage is probably having issues with their roof and may need
guidance on how to choose the right roofer in their city or county.
6. Add Related Posts
6. Add Related Posts
In HTML, you can add a related post by adding a hyperlink to the
actual title of the blog you want the user to read.
In WordPress, there are many plugins that help you show
related posts. One of the best plugins for related posts on the
market is Yuzo.


The longer your page takes to load, the shorter your visitors will stay there. Page
load time is a crucial factor both for CTR and in determining how long people stay
on your website.
If your website is the most attractive in the world and your content extremely terrific
and flawless, nobody will wait for websites that take several seconds to load.
According to Google, longer page load time has a negative impact on bounce rates
(the rate at which people leave your website).
In their words, Google says:
“For example, if your page load time increases from 1 second to 3 seconds, the
bounce rate increases 32 percent. If your page load time increases from 1 second to
6 seconds, bounce rate increases by 106 percent”
48
Install a plugin for cache
Optimize your images – not more than 100 bytes
Get a good hosting 
If you use WordPress, try the following strategies:
www.rankfortress.com
7. Optimize for High Performance
7. Optimize for High Performance
Reduce the dimension of background images
Implement lazy loading - images only load as the visitor scrolls down your page
Remove large page elements, such as large video files, bulky CSS file, and etc
There are several ways to increase your page load speed, including:


Many web visitors would rather watch a testimonial video rather than
read text-based content. More than half of executives surveyed said
they would rather watch a video than read text. Also a good number of
marketers that use video grow revenue 49 percent faster than non-
video users.
Adding video on your website might seem like a lot of work, but it’s
worth the try. Besides, video production doesn’t have to be as stressful
as it seems. You can easily repurpose one of your text-based pieces of
content into a presentation video or ask your customer to talk about
their experience with your product or service.
49
www.rankfortress.com
8. Use Engaging Videos
8. Use Engaging Videos


50
www.rankfortress.com
9. Use a Content Delivery Network (CDN)
9. Use a Content Delivery Network (CDN)
With more traffic coming to your website, your server can be severely overwhelmed.
A high degree of traffic spike can lead to server failure and hinder access to your
website. You can prevent this problem from happening by using a content delivery
network (CDN).
CDN ensures that your variable traffic doesn’t affect your server. It does this by
accelerating the speed of content delivery regardless of the user’s device or location
to ensure uninterrupted access to content.
The network service caches the files of your site and delivers the content to the user
through a service that is closest to them.

This chapter starts with an introduction to the subject of local SEO. Local search is a type of
INTERNET marketing that focuses on optimizing websites for both organic and paid searches
based on geographic information including zip codes, cities, counties and state names. For
example, when someone types in "best coffee shops near me" into Google or other search engine,
it's likely they'll see three lists: one for natural or organic results which are free listings found
Online; another list will show ads sponsored by companies who want to rank well in the paid results
and last but not least the listings found in Google Maps or better known as the Goggle 3 Pack.

Again, you have got to be on top off all these items if you want any chance in succeeding as an
agency, freelancer and/or small business owner. Everyone has the potential to dominate the

The problem with most business owners is for too long they have been lied to and taken
advantage of by the armchair SEO gurus, that have not got a clue of how to run a business
much less dominate the Google 3 Pack.
The reality is the only fault of the business owner is that they have trusted the SEO gurus with

Page 8
In a nutshell, the practice of local SEO is targeted at reaching potential customers who are
searching for specific products and services with a specific location.
With more and more customers turning to search engines like Google when looking for
products or services in their local area, it’s important for every small business to prioritize local
search engine optimization. But the question is, what is local SEO and why is it important for
your business?
Local SEO is a branch of SEO that focuses on optimizing a website to be found in local search
results. Content creation, on-page optimization and link building with a laser-focused, localized
intent are all part of local SEO efforts.
Local search engine optimization is about increasing search visibility and ranking for
businesses that serve local customers face-to-face. These include businesses with physical
locations, like a grocery store, or service-based businesses such as a dentist's office or a law
firm that operates throughout a certain geographic area.
Optimizing for local search is important, especially for brick-and-mortar businesses serving
specific towns, cities, regions, and even states. When done right, local SEO allows people to
find relevant information about your business quickly and easily, bringing them closer to
transaction.
What Is Local SEO?
What Is Google My Business? 
If you already have a business website, your free Google Business Profile can complement
your website by adding more visibility to your current business on Google search and Google
Maps. In fact, you can add a link to your website on your Business Profile.
Google My Business (GMB) is a free local business directory that allows you to promote your
Business Profile on Google Search and Maps free of charge. With your GMB account, you can
see and connect with your customers, post updates to your Business Profile and see how
customers are interacting with your business on Google.
The Foundation


It helps you engage with customers
It highlights important information about your business
You can use the GMB dashboard to gain key insights on your audience and local performance
Provide directions to your location
Verify that your business is the best choice
And the best part is that... it’s all FREE to use.
If you’re still not sure what a Google My Business listing is, here is an example of what GMB
listings look like in local search results.
It’s important to note that businesses that have been “verified” by Google are twice likely to be
trusted by potential customers. Google My Business for search engine optimization appeals to the
trust component of Google’s algorithm. It’s recommended that you always keep your information
updated and accurate as your business evolves. Also, keep a steady stream of reviews coming in
and respond to them. This will signal to Google and real humans who decide to look your business
up on Google that your business is active.
The other big factor in SEO is content. The more unique, quality content you publish about your
business the better it will rank on Google’s search engine results page (SERP). In addition to blog
posts and social media updates that mention your company name or products, create original
articles like reviews of a local restaurant for example. You can also include slideshows with photos
from past events and upload video interviews with staff members.
If you need help on how to create your listing, you can visit Google’s help page at Google My
Business Help. The resource contains eligibility guidelines and information on how to claim your
local business listing. The other best resource is of source my Google Help Group on Facebook as
well as My Google My Business Masterclass, we run it several times throughout the year every
quarter usually.
Here is what Google has to say about their GMB accounts:
“Google My Business is a free and easy-to-use tool for businesses and organizations to
manage their online presence across Google, including Search and Maps. By verifying and
editing your business information, you can both help customers find you and tell them the
story of your business.”
Maintaining your business presence on Google My Business is important for several
reasons:
Reference: https://support.google.com/business/answer/3038063/about-google-my-
business-android?co=GENIE.Platform%3DAndroid&hl=en
Page 9


A local SEO 3-Pack is the listing of three businesses that come up in the search results when
searching for terms using keywords like “near me” or “near [location].” When a potential customer
searches for a local business, Google generates a list of potential businesses that fit your search
query. Being featured in the 3-Pack increases your chances of getting the most calls and clicks
from potential customers. Now the question is, does your business show up in the 3-Pack when
customers search for your business, or a product or service likes yours?
GMB is typically different from traditional SEO and Google Ads (formerly known as Google
Adwords). The magic of GMB is on mobile devices as results are often determined by the location
of the device. Good understanding of how close the person that is searching is to your location and
this becomes an important factor to determine whether you will rank in the 3-Pack or not.
Google ensures that best and most relevant results are delivered to the users based on proximity
and certain “hidden” ranking factors. You will learn some of these ranking factors in this book. The
primary objective of SEO is to help ensure that your business appears more relevant to Google
than your competitors. The trick to achieve this is by making sure that your website and GMB are
set up to meet the ranking requirements that Google is looking for.
Example of Google 3-Pack listings
There are a number of steps you can take to make your GMB list relevant to localized search
intent. Unfortunately many businesses are either not aware of these steps or they are not doing
them right. The list below contains some of the most fundamental things that you need to do right
to position your business as one of the most relevant in your industry or niche:
Ensure your listing is verified
Make sure your profile is 100% complete
Target keywords in your descriptions
Use a wide range of keyword-optimized images
Include at least one keyword-optimized video
Aim at more citations than your competitors (uber-important)
Drive more backlinks to your listing and citations (i.e. links from other websites – this passes
“authority” on to your listing and Makes it rank higher)
Keep getting a lot of genuine REVIEWS
Page 10


One of the key advantages of GMB over local SEO is that your listing can also show up for national
and global searches if your GMB is properly optimized and with potential customers searching for
your business nearby. If done right, GMB can be a game changer for your business as it has the
potential to bring more businesses to your brick-and-mortar store without running expensive ads.
This is what Google has to say about how it determines Local Rankings in GMB:
“The search phrases only become visible when an online user does a local Google search. For
instance, the user uses the search keywords “plumber near me” or “local plumbing company.”
The search result pages will display search results that include some local businesses. ”
Additional Resources
If you’re looking to learn more about local SEO and Google My Business, here’s an additional
resource designed to help you better understand the topics discussed in this section and the rest of
the book.
Page 11


You have a higher chance of ranking for local searches if your business name contains a
city where you operate and the primary service you provide or what is known as your
primary keyword. Therefore, consider using a city, county or town name and specific
service as part of your business name. Hence, “Perfect Plumbing Houston” instead of
“Perfect” or “Perfect Plumbing”.
Having a physical location such as a storefront or office will have a positive impact on
your local rankings and visibility. 
Including your local telephone number on your Business Profile tells potential customers
that you are truly a local business and helps you build trust. Additionally, this will help
remove confusion and increase confidence about your credibility. 
Having multiple GMB's in your city and/or surrounding areas is also a key factor in
dominating more areas and dominating 20-50 miles out.
To get started with local SEO, you need to familiarize with some ranking concepts.
Understanding these concepts and how they affect your ranking online will help you succeed
in your digital marketing efforts. That said, it is essential to have a good grasp of these
concepts before you venture into implementing your SEO strategy.
Below are some of the top facts you need to know about local SEO
The key here is to think outside of the box when it comes to ranking in the 3 pack and to test
our your theories. To many so called SEOs will tell you of that does not work or this does not
work but the reality is the way they do things does not work. 
TEST TEST TEST and ADJUEST = SUCCESS!
Chapter 
Getting Started with Local SEO
Page 12


Common Myths concerning Local SEO
For some years, certain myths have surrounded the practice of local SEO. The following are
some of the myths and facts about local SEO you need to know as you 
5 Local SEO Myths:
Page 13
 Local Rankings are increased in the 3 pack whenever you run a Google ad campaign.
1.
This commonly held belief turns out to be partly false. SEO (organic traffic) and Ads (paid
traffic) are two unrelated practices. Thus, one has no direct impact on the other. However that
being said if you use ads to run a Click Through Rate Manipulation campaign and do it
successfully it can be completely true. We will be covering CTR manipulation in a later
chapter.
2.  Rankings and Local Rankings are independent of each other.
In reality, organic rankings can have a considerable impact on your local rankings. If you have
impressive organic rankings, there are changes that your local ranking will improve with time.
However that being said I have ranked clients in the 3 pack with no traditional website at all.
3. You do local SEO only once and after that you will never have to do it again to
maintain your rankings.
Global SEO and local SEO are based on 2 completely different algorithms. However what
they both have in common is you will always need to do SEO. You will always need to do
some form of SEO to maintain your rankings in highly competitive areas but maybe not in less
competitive areas.
If you are a brick and mortar business that has a physical location, you will also need to do
Local SEO. Google uses algorithms (computer code) to decide how the search engine results
page should rank different websites for various searches. Think of it as an automated list of
your website’s social proof – all based on what they see in their algorithm.
In order to be successful with rankings, there is more than just Search Engine Optimization
involved. Social media marketing or digital Press Release campaigns can help bring traffic
back to your site from popular sites like Yelp, YouTube and Facebook if done correctly.


Page 14
4. Quick Wins are sustainable ranking positions
Quick wins does not guarantee long term success when it comes to SEO, generally speaking.
You can succeed in tricking search engines once or twice, but you can’t always have your
way. It is best to practice SEO skills that are evergreen and stand the test of time.
SEO is a game of consistency, it is not a magic pill even though some So called SEO
Magicians would like you to believe there is. The more you build your SEO on sustainable
strategy, the better for your business. As with anything digital marketing, consistency is key to
success in SEO.
5. Your Google My Business rankings are improved with geotagging your photos
This is half truth and half myth, seeing as when you upload images to Google they are indeed
removing any geodata attached to the images and thereby makes it a useless practice
ontoGMB specifically.
However you can still optimize the images on other sites such as your own with geodata
andyou can also do so on Press Releases and many other sites that do not remove
suchgeodata.


SEO performance is primarily data-driven and proximity driven, and understanding this will
help you channel the right efforts in your local SEO campaign. By data-driven, it means there
are literally analyses and explanations for everything from your ranking to traffic and dwell
time. In most cases, ranking fluctuations can also be explained based on experience
andmany times depending on Google Algo updates. This is once again why I teach all my
student to TEST TEST TEST and ADJUST. Because too many will tell you what does and
does not work and many times what they are telling you is complete garbage. I had to learn
the hard way over 20 years, so let me save you have the heart-ache.
With many Google updates going on almost every day and yearly, it can be tricky sometimes
to base your conclusion on rationality or what some so called Guru tells you. Once again
without you trying and testing different processes out you will not know especially if you listen
to so called Gurus or SEO Magicians.
Variance has a considerable impact since you have incomplete information. Creating and
implementing a long-term strategy can help you defeat uncertainty. Additionally, you have to
be patient while dealing with fluctuations. You need to take the time to test each case before
making assumptions about an event. Otherwise, it will be difficult to arrive at a trusted, and
informed conclusion which may result in recurring errors in the future.
Page 15


Here are some of the local ranking factors:
Proximity
Proximity is one of the most influential factors in local ranking. When setting up your GMB it is
best to visit your local city WIKI link to see exact Latitude and longitude and try your best to
get an address near those coordinates. We have tested this time and again and found that the
closer to the exact Latitude and longitude the better your rankings will be.
When ranking local businesses, Google selects businesses that are very close to the
searchers. This implies that the closer the searchers are to your physical business locations,
the more likely for you to rank first on the search results.
Part of the things you will gain from this eBook is strategy on how to expand your ranking
area. For example, you can improve your ranking by opening new office locations or
storefronts in areas where you want to get higher ranking.
Page 16
Google works with a large pool of data which enables them to accurately decide which result
should show up for which search query. As with global ranking, rankings in local SEO are
influenced by a number of factors which changes constantly. So if your going to get into the
game of SEO you have to find a reliable mentor and be ready to learn on a constant basis. Its
not a one time learning process, its literally a daily process.


Relevance is a key ranking factor used by the major search engines to deliver quality results
to their users. Google, for instance, wants to make sure that the businesses that come up fora
specific keyword actually matches the search query. This is where search content comes to
play. If the searcher is looking for a local plumber, they expect to see plumbers in the result.
Many factors can influence relevance such as the GMB dashboard category, description ,
reviews content press releases and blog content on your website.
Prominence, also known as authority, is an important ranking factor in search engines. A
business that has real life popularity, powerful brand and massive reaches will be considered
prominent and therefore ranked higher than others. Prominence is based on information that
Google has about your business across the web.
You can enhance your website’s prominence or authority through mentions and backlinks
from reputable websites within your industry. Some strategies for link building include press
release, citations, social media mentions, guest posting and many more.
Relevance
Prominence
Page 17


Page 18


1
GMB Business Signals - this encompasses several factors including business
title keywords, category, proximity and relevance. This factor constitutes 25.12%
of the total ranking score.
A Great tool for this is GMB Crush which breaks down everything from:
Categories, to word counts, images etc….That way you can literally reverse
engineer your GMB as well based on your findings.
2
 Link Signals – Link signals include the referring domain authority, inbound
anchor text, and quality of the domain name. This takes up to 16.53% of the total
score.
3
Review Signals – this includes review quality and quantity, review diversity and
review velocity. Another factor is to always use location + keyword in the reviews
you're receiving and always in your responses as well on the reviews. Review
signals take up to 15.44%.
4
5
On-Page Signals – This encompasses everything on the website, ranging from
keywords optimization, geotags, internal linking, neighborhood pages, alt tags,
and presence of NAP (Name Address & Phone Number). The on-page signals
constitute up to 13.82% of the local ranking score.
Citation Signals – Citations refer to the mentions generally on other websites.
Google ranks you online based on factors like citation volume, and data
aggregators NAP consistency/IYP. This factor influences 10.8% of your local
ranking.
6
Behavioral Signals – Customers’ activities on your Business Profile or website
are also an important ranking factor. For example, Google takes note of activities
such as check-ins, mobile clicks, click-through rate, and calls. This makes up to
9.56% of the core ranking factors.
7
8
Personalization – This takes about 5.88 of the local ranking factors.
Personalization options, including area-specific targeting helps Google not only to
understand what you are selling but also ascertain the exact locale of your
business.
Social Signals – Does your post often generate engagement or shares on social
media such as Facebook, Twitter or YouTube? Google looks at all these factors to
rank your website for local SEO. Social signals take 2.82% of the local ranking
factors.
 
The following ranking factors are based on rule of thumb; they will work for you if
you approach them correctly:
Page 19


Thanks to today’s modern technology, it’s now faster and easier to promote your
business or your company’s product or service using Google My Business. According
to a study by Search Engine Watch, 50% of the people who use mobile phones for
local searches end up visiting a physical store.
Considering the fact that almost everyone uses a smartphone nowadays, you can
imagine the potential sales you can get just through organic search and Google My
Business. That said, it is in your best interest to have a Google My Business Account
that is properly set up. Doing this will dramatically increase your chances of helping
customers to easily find your business when they search on Google.
PART 2
Page 20


According to Google: “Local results favor the most relevant results for each search, and
businesses with complete and accurate information are easier to match with the right searches.”
This brings to the reason why you need to ensure that your GMB Profile isn’t only properly set up
but also optimized for relevance. When properly optimized, it showcases your best features and
makes it easy for consumers to discover, learn about and contact your business. But before
delving deep into your Business Profile optimization, let’s talk about how you can set up your
account in a simple, straight to the point manner.

GMB Profile Setup

Your GMB is an account while your Google Business Profile is your free listing on Google that
appears on Google Maps, the local results of Google Search and the right-hand knowledge
Panel of Google Search. To make the most out of your business profile, you need to input all
the relevant information that distinguishes you from the competition. In addition, the
information you provide must be accurate to avoid credibility issues with customers.
Messaging Setup 
To do this, head over to Google Play Store or App store for the Google My Business app and
download it on your device. Once downloaded, make sure you turn on the Messaging feature.
You will see a “Request a Quote” button, which will be visible on your profile in Google. This
will only appear if you have an industry that is eligible to earn that button.
Page 22


In the following image, you will notice the local search rankings. This image includes three
GMB listings, and each of the listings provides valuable information such as the name of the
business, telephone number, location, hours, and review count.
Conversely, businesses without a storefront have to omit their addresses on Google. In this
case, the only information that will be visible to customers is their service areas. Good is
particularly keen on ensuring that customers only visit the store and not going to an office.
